rationale: The citing paper’s design centers on a dynamic market-share attraction system with SUR estimation, while the cited paper develops a coupled dynamic share/distribution model (also estimated with SUR) for market-structure emergence. This strong conceptual/methodological overlap suggests the citation could be serving as a substantive theory or modeling precedent, not just a generic mention. With only DOI-level match evidence but plausible substantive use, it should be kept for second-stage review.
